content.claim: A 2023 survey of 121 Hungarian primary teachers found that 83.6% reported an increase in students requiring developmental support, with teachers graduating in 2010 or later more likely to have encountered SEN students.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Demography lens
limitations: Based on perceived prevalence (subject to awareness and labeling bias), not population-based incidence measurement
Small sample size (121 teachers) not representative at national level
Cannot be treated as independent confirmation of a 'real' rise in SEN incidence
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
